title: Luffy Runs Toward Food
description: On pages 9–12, this part focuses on luffy runs toward food, showing the key character choices and conflict behind this chapter beat.
scene: Luffy Runs Toward Food. The Straw Hats reach Alabasta and immediately see the public admiration for Crocodile, the Warlord behind the kingdom’s crisis. The scene description focuses on what the reader needs to understand before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Alabasta citizen
dialogue.text: He beat us to the attack again?

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: by the time we arrived at Nanohana, the pirate attack was already...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: As long as the citizens are okay, I suppose it's fine...

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: I don't know what you're talkin' about.

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: What's with your shitty-eyes then? Look me straight in the eyes.

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Now let me ask you one thing.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I properly divided our food supplies so that they'd last long enough for 8 people to reach Alabasta.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: If you're not responsible, then why's the food disappearing every night?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Don't even try to make any excuses. You're a terrible liar.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hey, is that some leftovers I see stuck to your mouth?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Nami, did you see? Just look at what this buffoon did! Won't you buy me a refrigerator with a lock on it? (D)

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Hmm, I'll think about it. After all, it is a matter of life and death...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: GULF... Guess we should really catch something,

dialogue.speaker: Alabasta citizen
dialogue.text: means that you're a pirate employed by the world government itself.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: So whether it's a shichi- bukai who gets rid of the railing pirates,

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Or the Marines who do it for justice,

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: The end result is the same.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: The people are thankful to whoever kept them safe.

summary: This part highlights luffy runs toward food and moves the chapter forward through its central emotional or plot turn.
